https://drive.google.com/file/d/1G7DSltB.title: Bucket List Family - 30 Days at Disney World

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1MHnYG3Z.title: Chip and Dale decorate for Christmas
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1MHnYG3Z.caption: Chip and Dale string lights, hang wreaths, and generally cause chaos while decorating Disney's Animal Kingdom for the holidays. Produced as part of a six-year run on Disney-branded shorts and seasonal spots — quick-turn, character-driven, and built to play across Disney's social and broadcast channels. Pure holiday mischief, exactly the speed those two work at.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Urd1yHH.caption: Trendy glowing swag built for the 20-something Disney World crowd—light-up ears, reactive accessories, after-dark merch designed to pop under park lights. I produced the spot during my Disney run, packaging the product hits into a fast, club-energy edit that sells the night-mode vibe without spelling it out. Hit play and you'll feel the BPM.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1UkF8q1j.caption: Trend-driven must-dos at Walt Disney World, cut for the twenty-something crowd chasing the next viral moment. Producer chair: pitched the beats, scouted the parks, and shaped each piece into something that travels on social. Made under the WDW Happy Place banner during my 2015–2021 run.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1CGE2rMb.title: Andy Night at Toy Story Land
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1CGE2rMb.caption: Fifty guys named Andy — including a few famous ones — got the keys to Toy Story Land for one surreal overnight takeover. Producing this one meant solving the logistics of locking down a Disney park after hours, casting an entire guest list by first name, and building a night that played like Andy's bedroom come to life. The result is the kind of brand stunt that only works if every detail lands.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1OfS_lg2.caption: Brought Cache Bunny — one of Instagram's sharpest visual storytellers — into Walt Disney World to chase a single iconic frame. We scouted, timed the light, and walked away with a wall-spin shot that traveled. Press play to see how it landed.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1zh3MWLz.title: Adventurer Ed Stafford at Animal Kingdom
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1zh3MWLz.caption: Brought Ed Stafford — the first man to walk the Amazon — to Disney's Animal Kingdom to test the park against the real wild he's known for. Followed him through the attractions, animal encounters, and behind-the-scenes corners most guests never see. Part travelogue, part stunt-cast experiment, all built around watching a hardened explorer react in real time. Hit play to see how the jungle reads to someone who's actually lived in one.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1VrzQAup.title: WDW Birthday Greeting
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1VrzQAup.caption: A personalized video greeting that lit up the in-room TV when a guest checked into Walt Disney World on their birthday. Produced the piece across a six-year run — scripting the beats, directing voice talent, and coordinating with park ops to make sure the moment landed the second the door opened. Small touch, big reaction.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/10h6W7Eq.caption: Sarge and the Green Army Men whip Orlando City SC into shape on the Toy Story Land turf — boot camp meets Major League Soccer, pure Pixar mischief. Produced for Disney as part of the Toy Story Land launch push, blending IP, athletes, and a wink at every kid who's ever staged a plastic-soldier ambush. Hit play.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1vDKPf3Z.caption: Olivia Rodrigo and Sean Gambrione spin through the Mad Tea Party at Disneyland to tee up the Disney Channel Holiday Celebration. Produced as a promo spot during my run on the Disney park-side production team — short, sweet, and built to land in 60 seconds. Hit play.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1wAEXhYl.title: Disney Family's Backyard Theater
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1wAEXhYl.caption: Backyard Theatre is a Target-sponsored Disney Channel interstitial I pitched, directed, and produced — kid-sized toys reenacting the Woody-versus-Buzz bedroom showdown in a real backyard. The cut hits the iconic beats fast: the spaceship-in-my-spot jab, the to-infinity launch, then a soft handoff to Target's creativity tag. Tight pacing, clean rhythm, brand sign-off in under thirty.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1wfesqgo.caption: Brandon Baker reprises Johnny Tsunami — surfboard and all — for an Oh My Disney Vine, except this time he's clocking in at a cubicle. Directed and produced for Disney's social team, with the joke landing in six seconds flat.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1wTfxHKI.caption: Hundreds of Tsum Tsum plushies, frame by frame, marching down a miniature Main Street U.S.A. — a stop-motion parade staged for Disney's social channels. I directed and produced the spot end to end, from rig design to the final composite. It's tiny, it's chaotic, and every step took hours.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1b9C_-DD.caption: Random acts of holiday magic at the Disney Store — surprise giveaways dropped on unsuspecting shoppers, cut for maximum gasp-and-grin reactions. Edited the piece to ride the energy of each reveal, letting the guests' faces carry the moment. A short, warm watch that captures what Share the Magic was actually about.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1iTlo9sG.caption: Behind-the-scenes look at how an Olaf Disney Infinity figure goes from concept to shelf — sculpt, paint, packaging, the whole pipeline. Cut for the Unboxing Frozen series during my time editing for Disney. Hit play to watch the figure come together piece by piece.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1iQRpASR.caption: A nature-doc parody following Tsum Tsums in the wild — narrated stalk-and-pounce footage of a Goofy plush trying to outrun a very real cat. Cut as a recurring short series during my Disney run, leaning into David Attenborough cadence and tight comedic timing. Episode 1 of Tsum Tsum Kingdom.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1z7V5hY6.title: Making a Sled Dolly for a Marvel Film
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1z7V5hY6.caption: Behind-the-scenes cut documenting the build of a custom sled dolly used on a Marvel-branded production. Edited from my own footage for my YouTube channel — process, problem-solving, and the rig in motion. Hit play to see how it came together.

https://www.instagram.com/artios_sugarhi.caption: Tech-week BTS cut for a private high school's Little Mermaid production — fish-out-of-water energy, costume fittings, and a cast that's clearly having too much fun. Edited from on-the-ground footage to give parents and prospective families a peek behind the curtain before opening night. Produced and cut as part of the school's content and social marketing reel.
